schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between share valuation and business valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Business valuation determines enterprise value (value of the entire business). Share valuation derives per-share value from enterprise value by adjusting for debt, cash, and dividing by total diluted shares. For complex capital structures (CCPS, convertibles), share valuation requires waterfall/OPM modelling beyond simple division.

mainEntity.name: Which method does Rule 11UA prescribe for share valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: For unquoted equity shares: the higher of NAV method and DCF method. The DCF must be certified by a CA or SEBI Merchant Banker. For listed shares: closing market price on the valuation date.

mainEntity.name: How do you value CCPS (Compulsorily Convertible Preference Shares)?

acceptedAnswer.text: CCPS valuation uses Option Pricing Model (OPM) or Probability-Weighted Expected Return Method (PWERM). The OPM treats each share class as a call option on the enterprise value with breakpoints determined by liquidation preferences. Our engine models the full waterfall including anti-dilution provisions.

Share transfer (Section 56(2)(x) IT Act), FEMA transactions (resident to non-resident), ESOP grants (Ind AS 102), Companies Act requirements (Section 62, 230, 232), buyback of shares, and investor transactions (fundraising, exits).

mainEntity.name: What methods are used for share valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Income approach: DCF, Dividend Discount Model. Market approach: Comparable Company Analysis, Comparable Transaction. Asset approach: NAV, Adjusted Book Value. Choice depends on purpose and regulatory requirement.

mainEntity.name: Is share valuation needed for gift of shares?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Under Section 56(2)(x), if shares are received as gift and FMV exceeds ₹50,000: excess is taxable as income of recipient. Valuation per Rule 11UA required to determine FMV.

mainEntity.name: Can share valuation be challenged by tax authorities?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. AO can reject valuation if assumptions are unreasonable. Most disputes centre on: DCF growth rate, discount rate, and terminal value assumptions. Our Monte Carlo approach provides defensible probability-based results.
